required_edits: Fix or justify the mutation-boundary hole in readonlySet: the facade is a plain object, not a real Set, and it is NOT actually immutable against the spec's stated threat model. `Object.freeze(facade)` freezes the facade's own properties, but the underlying private `set` is reachable via the bound closures only — however the facade itself can still be subverted via `Object.getPrototypeOf(facade)` (Object.prototype) pollution and, more importantly, the facade fails `instanceof Set` and `Set.prototype.has.call` interop. At minimum: (a) set the facade's prototype to null or Set.prototype-compatible shape, and (b) add a test asserting the underlying set cannot be reached/mutated (e.g., that `NODE_KINDS.size` is non-writable and mutators throw even after `Object.getPrototypeOf` manipulation). If the council deems plain-object facade acceptable, document the deliberate choice in a comment tied to the task line 'do not use Object.freeze(new Set(...))'.

deriveRepositoryRef violates the frozen shallow-guard specification's error semantics: the spec says 'require the output to be exactly `false`, otherwise throw the stable shallow-repository error'. The implementation conflates malformed output (e.g. 'yes', empty string) with genuine shallowness by throwing ShallowRepositoryError for both — this matches the letter of 'otherwise throw the stable shallow-repository error' so it is arguably compliant, BUT the task's unit list separately names 'malformed non-`false` output' as a distinct branch to cover, and the test at section 12 asserts ShallowRepositoryError for malformed output, cementing the conflation. Confirm with the spec owner that malformed-output => ShallowRepositoryError is intended; if malformed output must be a distinct fatal error, split the branch and update the test. Record the resolution either way.

The real-git fixture builder does not use 'the existing no-shell wrapper' as the task requires ('using only allowlisted `git` commands through the existing no-shell wrapper'). The test invokes execFileSync('git', ...) directly. execFileSync is no-shell in effect, but the task names an existing wrapper; either route the fixture's git calls through that wrapper (if it exists in the repo at this point) or record an explicit authorized deviation noting the wrapper lands in Task 4a (as the registry.mjs comment itself claims) and that direct execFileSync is the interim mechanism.

Add the missing spec-mandated locator/edge coverage: (1) a rejection test for missing `repository_ref` and missing content hash on EACH repository-scoped kind (the suite only exercises repository-file for missing-field cases; the task text demands 'missing `repository_ref` or content hash on each repository-scoped kind'); (2) 'empty provenance' rejection is listed in the task's reject list but no test exercises an empty/blank source_ref beyond the empty string — verify empty-provenance semantics are fully covered or add the case; (3) short-SHA rejection is tested only via blob_sha 'abc' on repository-file — add short-SHA cases for text_sha256/entry_hash/summary_sha256 bearing kinds or confirm the shared requireHex path makes per-kind repetition unnecessary and note that in the test comments.

validateAssertionStatus accepts any assertor matching STABLE_ID that starts with 'model:' — but 'model:' contains ':' which STABLE_ID permits mid-string, while requireAssertor runs BEFORE the coupling check, so 'model:' alone (trailing colon, no model name) passes STABLE_ID ('m' start, ':' allowed) and maps to model-proposal. Add a rejection (or explicit acceptance) test for degenerate assertors 'model:' and 'model: ' to pin the boundary; if 'model:' bare should be rejected, add the guard.
hard_stops: Scope expansion: registry.mjs exports WEAVER_IDS as a fourth public read-only registry. The frozen Task 2 interface list names exactly three registries (NODE_KINDS, EDGE_KINDS, ASSERTION_STATUS) plus nine functions; WEAVER_IDS is not in the interface. The five weaver ids are needed internally for status coupling, but exporting them as a public registry (and testing WEAVER_IDS.size===5 as if it were spec) creates a new authoritative membership surface not authorized by v12 Task 2. Make WEAVER_IDS module-private (non-exported) or obtain explicit scope authorization; the test must then stop importing it.

Reinterpretation of the endpoint matrix without exhibited authority: the frozen Task 2 text specifies the four depends-on rows and the exact discharges matrix, but does NOT enumerate the introduced-by, verified-by, documented-in, motivated-by, or evidenced-by rows in the excerpt provided to this review. The implementation hard-codes motivated-by as ONLY code-symbol->concern and evidenced-by as ONLY code-symbol->run-evidence, and documented-in/verified-by as code-symbol|repository-file sourced. If these rows come from spec v2.3 they must be cited; as reviewed, they are unverifiable against the frozen scope and a wrong row here silently forbids/permits graph edges. Provide the spec v2.3 matrix citation for every non-depends-on/non-discharges row, or the matrix cannot be certified as faithful.

Uncited validation semantics presented as spec: several concrete rules are not derivable from the frozen Task 2 text supplied — (a) run-evidence.path must start with 'docs/runs/'; (b) code-symbol.symbol must match a JavaScript identifier regex; (c) asserted_by max length 128 and STABLE_ID charset; (d) heading normalization = NFC+trim+whitespace-collapse; (e) supersedes requires same-kind endpoints and human/model-only assertor. Each of these may well be in spec v2.3 or elsewhere in v12, but none is exhibited to this council. Faithful-implementation certification requires the authoritative source for each rule; otherwise these are reinterpretations. Supply citations or strip/relax to what Task 2 states.
rationale: The core engineering is strong: the private-Set facade honors the letter of the 'no Object.freeze(new Set())' instruction; canonicalJson implements sort/reject rules with cycle/sparse/non-plain/bigint/non-finite rejection and order-independence + array-order-sensitivity tests; deriveNodeId hashes canonicalJson({kind,locator}) exactly; the four depends-on rows and the exact discharges matrix (code-symbol->obligation, obligation->contract-clause, others rejected) match the frozen text verbatim; content bindings per kind (blob_sha / text_sha256 / entry_hash / summary_sha256) and the commit-only global exception match; deriveRepositoryRef invokes exactly the two specified git commands with exact-'false' and exactly-one-40-hex-root requirements; the D18 real-git fixture builds a two-commit origin, proves shallow rejection and full-clone git-root equality, cleans up in finally; injected units cover all four named branches; the deterministic gate (zero deps, node:-only imports, diff confined to clotho/) passed. However, I cannot sign 'faithful within frozen scope' as-is. Two categories block: (1) WEAVER_IDS is exported as a fourth public registry — a genuine interface-surface expansion beyond the frozen three-set-plus-nine-function contract, trivially fixable by un-exporting; (2) a substantial fraction of the enforced semantics (five of eight endpoint-matrix rows, run-evidence path prefix, symbol identifier syntax, assertor charset/length, heading normalization, supersedes assertor policy) is not present in the Task 2 text exhibited to this review. Those rules are plausibly correct transcriptions of spec v2.3 — the task text itself references 'the endpoint rules above' and 'the exact spec v2.3 discharges matrix', implying a fuller matrix exists upstream — but a faithfulness review cannot certify rules it cannot see the authority for. This is why the decision is 'revise' rather than 'reject': the fix path is citation/authorization plus one export removal, not redesign. The required edits also capture smaller gaps: per-kind missing-field coverage is thinner than the task's explicit 'each repository-scoped kind' demand; the fixture bypasses the named no-shell wrapper (execFileSync direct); malformed shallow-output conflated with ShallowRepositoryError should be confirmed as intended; the 'model:' degenerate assertor boundary is unpinned; and the facade's immutability posture deserves one hardening test. Gate result is necessary but not sufficient — it checks file presence, string presence, and green tests, none of which detect scope expansion or uncited semantics.
